title: Performance & Deployment

questions.question_text: What is the purpose of 'minification' in the build process?
options: To make the code more readable
To reduce file size for faster loading
questions.explanation: Minification removes unnecessary characters from code without changing its functionality, resulting in smaller files that load faster over the network.

options: Breaking code into two separate files
Dynamic loading of code chunks only when needed to reduce initial bundle size
questions.explanation: Vite and Webpack handle this by creating separate .js files for different routes or large libraries.

questions.question_text: Which React component works with React.lazy to show a loading state during a split chunk download?
questions.explanation: Suspense takes a 'fallback' prop (usually a spinner).

questions.question_text: What is the benefit of 'Memoization' (React.memo) for components?
options: It prevents a component from re-rendering if its props haven't changed
It saves them to the database
It hides them from the user
questions.explanation: Great for large lists or complex components used frequently.

questions.question_text: What does a 'Production Build' do to your code?
options: Minifies code, removes comments, and optimizes assets for the smallest possible size
questions.explanation: Production builds are much faster for the user to download.

questions.question_text: Which command is standard for creating a production build in a Vite/React app?
questions.explanation: The output is usually placed in a '/dist' or '/build' folder.

options: Automatically removing unused code from the final bundle
questions.explanation: Modern tools only include the functions you actually `import`.

questions.question_text: Where is a common place to host a modern React frontend for free?

options: A way to store sensitive keys (like API URLs) that change between development and production
A secret password for the user
questions.explanation: Never commit sensitive .env values to GitHub!

questions.question_text: What is the purpose of 'Lighthouse' in Chrome DevTools?
options: To audit the Performance, Accessibility, and SEO of your website
questions.explanation: Aim for high scores to ensure a premium user experience.

options: Sending a pre-rendered HTML page from the server to the browser for better SEO and perceived speed
Rendering code on the user's phone
questions.explanation: Frameworks like Next.js are popular for SSR.

options: A website that is always updating
A website that can be installed on a home screen and work offline like an app
questions.explanation: PWAs use 'Service Workers' to cache files offline.

questions.question_text: Which attribute on a <script> tag helps improve page load speed?
questions.explanation: These prevent the script from blocking the initial HTML rendering.

questions.question_text: How do you avoid 'Large Component Trees' becoming slow?
options: Component splitting and keeping state local as much as possible
questions.explanation: Only update what needs to be updated.

questions.question_text: What is 'Hydration' in the context of SSR?
options: The process of React 'attaching' its logic to static HTML already sent by the server
questions.explanation: Hydration makes the static page interactive.

questions.question_text: What does a 'Content Delivery Network' (CDN) do?
options: Distributes your website files across servers worldwide to serve them from the closest location to the user
questions.explanation: CDNs make your site load fast everywhere on Earth.
